subject: REVISION: photonic transport — ZMQ topic replaces UDP multicast proposal

body.summary: Revising photonic Phase 2 transport from UDP multicast to ZMQ pub/sub topic. meshd already runs ZMQ for heartbeat gossip and peer discovery — adding a photonic topic reuses existing infrastructure instead of building new.

revision.original: UDP multicast listener in meshd (new transport layer, M effort)
revision.revised: Add "photonic" topic to existing ZMQ pub/sub (reuse infrastructure, XS effort)
revision.rationale: meshd already handles ZMQ connect/disconnect, peer topology, and message routing. A dedicated topic filter adds 1 line of Go. No new ports, protocols, or listeners.

zmq_implementation.publisher: Emitter hook publishes photonic/v1 token to local meshd ZMQ PUB socket
zmq_implementation.topic: "photonic" — dedicated topic string, separate from heartbeat/gossip
zmq_implementation.subscriber: meshd SUB socket adds "photonic" to topic filter. On receive, write to /tmp/{peer}-photonic-state.json
zmq_implementation.frequency: Every 1-5 seconds during active processing (matches current local file write rate)
zmq_implementation.token_size: ~100 bytes JSON (photonic/v1 schema unchanged from turn 1)

what_ops_implements: Add "photonic" topic filter to existing ZMQ subscriber (~1 line)
On photonic message receive: write to /tmp/{sender}-photonic-state.json
Optional: expose photonic state in /api/status response for LCARS dashboard
body.brain_mapping_updated: docs/brain-architecture-mapping.md section 7 — implementation path revised, Phase 2 effort downgraded from M to XS

action_gate.gate_condition: Ops adds photonic topic to ZMQ subscriber

epistemic_flags: ZMQ pub/sub provides at-most-once delivery — photonic tokens may drop under load. Acceptable: dropped tokens carry no consequence (same as biological biophoton attenuation).
